content:
Character: Jamal Osei
Perception: Jamal's got presence — tall, steady hands, watchful eyes, the kind of face that photographs well without trying. He makes the best cortado in the Village, no exaggeration. He's observant and efficient, doesn't waste words, which I respect. I'm a 7 AM regular; he has my order memorized. I think he sees me as a predictable customer who tips well and doesn't linger too long. I know he's from somewhere in the boroughs, works hard, takes pride in the craft. We've exchanged maybe two hundred sentences total, but there's a rhythm to our mornings that I'd miss if it disappeared. Once I came in visibly hungover after a bad phone call with my father, and he set down the coffee without a word and didn't charge me. That's character.
summary:

I think he sees me as a predictable customer who tips well and doesn't linger too long. I know he's from somewhere in the boroughs, works hard, takes pride in the craft. We've exchanged maybe two hundred sentences total, but there's a rhythm to our mornings that I'd miss if it disappeared. Once I came in visibly hungover after a bad phone call with my father, and he set down the coffee without a word and didn't charge me. That's character.
